perturbation.original_text: You shall submit to us copies of all Unit Franchise Agreements executed with Unit Franchisees within ten (10) days of the date of their execution, together with a copy of all checks presented to you at closing. You shall prepare and submit to us a monthly report, not later than the first (1 s t ) day of each month, of Franchise Sales Revenue generated by you during the previous calendar month. Contemporaneously with the submission of the Franchise Sales Revenue report, you shall prepare and submit to us a monthly report, in such form and including such detail as we require, reflecting royalty fees paid and owed to you by your Unit Franchisees for the previous calendar month. Any report not actually received by us when due shall be deemed overdue and you shall pay us a late charge as described above.
perturbation.changed_text: You may submit to us copies of all Unit Franchise Agreements executed with Unit Franchisees within thirty (30) days of the end of each quarter, together with a summary report of all checks presented to you at closing during the quarter. You may prepare and submit to us a quarterly report, no later than the fifteenth (15th) day following each quarter, of Franchise Sales Revenue generated by you during the previous quarter. Contemporaneously with the submission of the Franchise Sales Revenue report, you may prepare and submit to us a quarterly report, in such form and including such detail as we require, reflecting royalty fees paid and owed to you by your Unit Franchisees for the previous quarter. Any report received by us more than sixty (60) days after the end of a quarter shall be deemed overdue and you shall pay us a late charge as described above.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires monthly submission of Franchise Agreements and Sales Revenue reports while the modified text changes those to quarterly submissions, creating a contradiction because other clauses might still refer to monthly requirements. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the acceptable frequency of report submissions. The language change from `shall` to `may` introduces ambiguity regarding whether report submission is mandatory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You shall pay us all Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fees and Franchise Sales Royalty Fees, due under this Section 4 by electronic funds transfer by us against a bank account maintained by you. You agree to execute the documents required by us, our bank and/or your bank in order to permit us to conduct electronic funds transfers to and from your account, and you shall not close your account without our prior consent. Your failure to comply with the terms of this Section 4.3 shall be deemed to be a breach of this Agreement. You hereby authorize us to initiate debit entries and/or credit collection entries to your bank account for the payment of Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fees, Franchise Sales Royalty Fees, National Advertising Fund Contributions, and all other sums that may become due to us or our affiliates from you. You shall make funds available for withdrawal by us by electronic transfer on such dates of each month as we shall designate throughout the term of this Agreement. If you fail to provide the revenue reports described in Section 4.6 below, then in addition to the late fee described in such Section, we may debit your account for one hundred forty percent (140%) of the last Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee, Franchise Sales Royalty Fee and/or National Advertising Fund Contribution (as applicable) that we debited. If the Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee, Franchise Sales Royalty Fee and/or National Advertising Fund Contribution we debit are less than the fees you actually owe us, once we have been able to determine the true and correct revenue amounts, we will debit your account for the balance on a day we specify. If the Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee, Franchise Sales Royalty Fee and/or National Advertising Fund Contribution we debit are greater than the fees you actually owe us, we will credit the excess against the amount we otherwise would debit from your account for the next payment due.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the frequency of report submissions from 'monthly' to 'quarterly' and changes the obligation to submit reports from 'shall' to 'may'. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which states 'You shall make funds available for withdrawal by us by electronic transfer on such dates of each month as we shall designate throughout the term of this Agreement. If you fail to provide the revenue reports described in Section 4.6 below...'. The contradicted text assumes monthly revenue reports for calculating and debiting fees, but the modified text permits quarterly reports, introducing ambiguity and potentially preventing timely withdrawals.

perturbation.original_text: You shall maintain a safe and reasonably clean place of business in compliance with all governmental and industry standards and conduct the Franchised Business in a manner that generates goodwill and public approval of you and us.
perturbation.changed_text: You shall endeavor to maintain a reasonably safe place of business in general compliance with governmental guidelines, and conduct the Franchised Business in a manner that aims to generate goodwill, but the Franchisor recognizes results may vary.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates maintaining a safe and clean place of business in compliance with standards, whereas the changed text softens this obligation by stating 'endeavor to maintain' and 'general compliance,' introducing ambiguity. It also changes `generates goodwill` to `aims to generate goodwill` and recognizes results may vary. Other clauses of the contract expect strict compliance and adherence to standards, this perturbation weakens the enforcement of those standards by introducing discretion.The change from `shall` to `endeavor to` also removes a binding obligation to guarantee safety, which weakens other obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: YOUR OBLIGATIONS You understand and acknowledge that every detail of the System is essential to you and us in order to develop and maintain quality operating standards, to increase the demand for the products and services sold by all master franchisees operating under the System and to protect the Proprietary Marks and our reputation and goodwill. You shall comply with our standards with respect to services, products and operations and shall operate the Franchised Business in strict conformity with such methods, standards, and specifications as we may from time to time prescribe in the Manuals or otherwise. You shall refrain from deviating from such standards, specifications and procedures without our prior written consent and from otherwise operating in any manner which reflects adversely on the Proprietary Marks or the System. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, you agree that: (a) You shall only use and serve soups that are approved by us and must be purchased from suppliers designated or approved in writing by us and you acknowledge that all soups approved shall not be those of Al Yeganeh. We will use our best efforts to have all soups produced to Al Yeganeh's standards. Any deviation from the above shall result in default of the Agreement and grounds for immediate termination without opportunity to cure. (b) Soups shall be cost plus 25% FOB the supplier. (c) You hereby agree that you shall serve three (3) soups daily, in any format approved in writing by us, inside the kettles located in the front line of the restaurant. We prefer you serve daily six (6) soups in any format, approved in writing by us. (d) In order to keep your exclusivity, you agree to purchase a minimum of the following: $170,000.00 of soup from us in 2015 $1,600,00.00 of soup from us in 2016 $3,200,000.00 of soup from us in 2017 $5,000,000.00 of soup from us in 2018 and shall increase 10% each year thereafter. (e) We maintain the right to open and operate new company units and to sell franchises to our existing franchisees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation to maintain standards, which contradicts the strict compliance requirements outlined in the 'YOUR OBLIGATIONS' section. The original text states 'shall maintain', while the changed text uses 'endeavor to maintain' and 'general compliance'. This directly clashes with the requirement to 'operate the Franchised Business in strict conformity with such methods, standards, and specifications' and the franchisee 'shall refrain from deviating from such standards', creating a strong in-text contradiction because the original text is an obligation but the changed text is no longer an obligation.

perturbation.original_text: The annual meeting will be mandatory for all master franchisees, unless your absence is excused by us.
perturbation.changed_text: The annual meeting is strongly encouraged for all master franchisees, but attendance is at your discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the annual meeting is mandatory, while the modified version makes attendance discretionary. This introduces an in-text contradiction if any other part of the agreement assumes mandatory meeting attendance for compliance or training requirements. This changes the nature of the obligation and removes the requirement, which is a contradiction. Removing an element makes it a contridiction with other obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your employees shall also attend such additional courses, seminars, and other training programs as we may reasonably require from time to time. We shall not charge any fees for those attending these additional courses, seminars, or other training programs, but you shall be responsible for any and all other expenses incurred in connection with attending and sending your employees to such training programs including, without limitation, the costs of transportation, lodging, meals, training materials and any wages. We shall, in our sole discretion, select the time and location of all additional training programs.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates attendance at the annual meeting, establishing it as a compulsory requirement. The modified text changes this to "strongly encouraged" with attendance "at your discretion." This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which requires attendance at additional training programs, creating ambiguity about whether attendance is mandatory, thereby creating an in-text contradiction.
